name: Bengal famine of 1770
summary: Severe scarcity in eastern Bengal after crop failure; early Company-era famine often linked to revenue extraction and disrupted grain markets.

name: Madras/Bengal scarcity (1783–84)
summary: Drought-related shortages in the Madras Presidency and parts of Bengal amid rising land revenue demands.

name: Orissa famine (1865–67)
summary: Rice crop failures in Orissa and Bihar; relief was slow while grain continued to move toward export markets.

name: Great Famine (Deccan, 1876–78)
summary: Widespread failure of rain-fed crops across the Deccan and beyond, intensified where cotton commercialization had expanded on marginal land.

name: Indian famine (1896–97)
summary: All-India drought cycle; export-oriented districts faced higher prices as local grain was drawn into global commodity circuits.

name: Indian famine (1899–1900)
summary: Second late-century scarcity wave; regions already shifting to cash crops saw sharp food-price spikes.

name: Bengal famine of 1943
summary: Catastrophic food shortage in Bengal during WWII, amid jute and rice procurement policies and disrupted supply chains.
